data.body: Discovered during fix for 0e8c-a94d. config-paths.sh calls _cfg_read 6 times at source time, each spawning a separate read-config.sh subprocess (which itself does a git rev-parse). On cold disk (e.g. fresh temp dirs in tests), each subprocess takes ~0.1-0.4s, so config-paths.sh costs ~0.5-2.5s per compute-diff-hash.sh invocation. The compute-diff-hash.sh cold-start overhead also includes a checkpoint-walk loop of up to 10 git log + git rev-parse calls. Solution: consider using --batch mode of read-config.sh to read all config keys in a single subprocess, or cache CFG_* vars via a temp file keyed on git index mtime.
